\documentclass{article}
\usepackage{amsmath}

\title{A Paper with Code Repository}
\author{Alice Test}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
We introduce TestNet, a neural network for text classification.
Our code is available at https://github.com/test-user/testnet-repo.
\end{abstract}

\section{Introduction}
\label{sec:intro}

Text classification is a fundamental NLP task.
We present TestNet, a simple yet effective model.

\section{Methodology}
\label{sec:method}

Our model uses a two-layer transformer encoder followed by a classification head.
Implementation details and code are at https://github.com/test-user/testnet-repo.

\section{Results}
\label{sec:results}

TestNet achieves 92.3\% accuracy on the IMDB dataset and 88.1\% on SST-2.

\section{Conclusion}
\label{sec:conclusion}

We presented TestNet, achieving strong results on sentiment classification benchmarks.

\bibitem{devlin2019} Devlin, J. et al. (2019). BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers. Proc. of NAACL.
\end{thebibliography}

\end{document}
